volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: Epidicus (Section 1.1.1 - Section 1.1.35b)

sections.label: Section 1.1.1
sections.translation: Harkye ! young man .

sections.label: Section 1.1.1b
sections.translation: Who pulls me by the cloak , when thus in haste ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.2
sections.translation: An intimate .

sections.label: Section 1.1.2b
sections.original: Fateor , nam odio es nimium familiariter .
sections.translation: I confess it ; for with your annoyance you are too intimate .

sections.label: Section 1.1.3
sections.translation: But do look back , Thesprio !

sections.label: Section 1.1.3b
sections.translation: What ? Is it Epidicus that I see ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.5
sections.translation: Why surely you ’ ve the use of your eyes .

sections.label: Section 1.1.6
sections.translation: Greetings to you .

sections.label: Section 1.1.6b
sections.translation: May the Gods grant what you desire . I ’ m glad that you ’ ve got here safe .

sections.label: Section 1.1.7b
sections.translation: What besides ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.7c
sections.translation: According to the usage , a dinner shall be given you [*] .

sections.label: Section 1.1.8b
sections.translation: I agree .

sections.label: Section 1.1.8c
sections.translation: What to do ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.8d
sections.translation: That I ’ ll accept it , if you offer it .

sections.label: Section 1.1.9
sections.original: Quid tú agis ? ut vales ? exemplum adesse intellego . euge ,
sections.translation: How are you ? Fare you as you could wish ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.10
sections.translation: Marvellous ! [*] You seem quite plump and hearty .

sections.label: Section 1.1.10b
sections.translation: Thanks to this .

sections.label: Section 1.1.11
sections.translation: Which , indeed , you ought to have parted with [*] long ago .

sections.label: Section 1.1.12
sections.original: Mínus iam furtificus sum quam antehac .
sections.translation: I ’ m less of a pilferer now than formerly .

sections.label: Section 1.1.12b
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.12c
sections.translation: I rob above-board [*] .

sections.label: Section 1.1.13
sections.original: Di immortales te infelicent , ut tu es gradibus grandibus .
sections.translation: May the immortal Gods confound you , with what huge strides you do walk ! for when I caught sight of you at the harbour , I began to run at a rapid pace ;

sections.label: Section 1.1.15
sections.translation: I was hardly able to overtake you just now .

sections.label: Section 1.1.15b
sections.translation: You are a town wit .

sections.label: Section 1.1.15c
sections.translation: I know that you , on the other hand , are a military gentleman .

sections.label: Section 1.1.16b
sections.translation: Speak out as boldly as you please .

sections.label: Section 1.1.17
sections.translation: How say you ? Have you been well all along ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.17b
sections.translation: In a varied way .

sections.label: Section 1.1.17c
sections.translation: Those who are well in a varied way [*] , a race of men of the goat kind or of the panther kind , don ’ t please me .

sections.label: Section 1.1.19
sections.original: Quid tíbi vis dicam nisi quod est ? ut illaé res ? responde .
sections.translation: What do you wish me to tell you but that which is fact ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.19b
sections.translation: To answer to these things [*] fairly ;

sections.label: Section 1.1.20
sections.translation: how ’ s our master ’ s son ? Is he well ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.20b
sections.translation: Stout as a boxer and an athlete .

sections.label: Section 1.1.21
sections.original: Voluptábilem mihi nuntium tuo advéntu adportas , Thesprio .
sections.translation: You ’ ve brought me joyous tidings on your arrival . But where is he ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.22b
sections.translation: I came here together with him .

sections.label: Section 1.1.22c
sections.original: Vbi is ergo est ? nisi si in vidulo
sections.translation: Where is he then ? Unless , perchance , you ’ ve brought him in your wallet , or , perhaps , in your knapsack .

sections.label: Section 1.1.23b
sections.translation: May the Gods confound you !

sections.label: Section 1.1.23c
sections.translation: I want to make enquiries of you . Lend me your attention ; attention shall be lent you in return .

sections.label: Section 1.1.25
sections.translation: You say what ’ s law [*] .

sections.label: Section 1.1.25b
sections.translation: It becomes me to do so .

sections.label: Section 1.1.25c
sections.original: Iam tu autem nobis praeturam geris ?
sections.translation: But why now are you acting the Praetor over us ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.26
sections.original: Quem díces digniorem esse hominem hódie Athenis alterum ?
sections.translation: What other person in Athens will you say is more deserving of it than I ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.26a
sections.translation: But still , Epidicus , one thing is wanting for your Praetorship .

sections.label: Section 1.1.27b
sections.translation: What , pray ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.27c
sections.translation: You shall know ; two lictors [*] two osier bundles of twigs —

sections.label: Section 1.1.28b
sections.translation: Woe unto you ! But how say you —?

sections.label: Section 1.1.29b
sections.translation: What is it you ask ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.29c
sections.translation: Where are the arms [*] of Stratippocles ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.30
sections.translation: I ’ faith , they ’ ve gone over to the enemy .

sections.label: Section 1.1.30b
sections.translation: What , his arms ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.30c
sections.translation: Aye , and quickly too .

sections.label: Section 1.1.30a
sections.translation: Do you say that seriously ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.31a
sections.translation: Seriously I say it ; the enemy have got them .

sections.label: Section 1.1.32
sections.translation: By my troth , a disgraceful affair .

sections.label: Section 1.1.32b
sections.original: At iam ante alii fecerunt idem .
sections.translation: Still , before now , other persons have done the same . This affair will turn out to his honor [*] .

sections.label: Section 1.1.33b
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 1.1.33c
sections.translation: Because it has been so to others before .

sections.label: Section 1.1.34
sections.original: Mulciber , credo , arma fecit quaé habuit Stratippocles :
sections.translation: Mulciber , I suppose , made the arms which Stratippocles had ;

sections.label: Section 1.1.35
sections.translation: they flew over to the enemy [*] .

sections.label: Section 1.1.35b
sections.translation: Why , then , e ’ en let this son of Thetis lose them ; the daughters of Nereus will bring him others .
